extraction.one_line_summary: MILESI Lorenzo and DSM won the Barcelona TTT starting second of 22 teams, beating MovieStar by 0.5 seconds to fifth rider despite racing in darkness.
tactical_patterns: DSM started second of 22 teams, 26 seconds faster at 9.6km check, suggesting early tire choice advantage in wet darkness.
MovieStar lost by 0.5 seconds to fifth rider after MOSS Enrique sprinted first, revealing TTT positioning discipline issues under pressure.
EVENEPOEL Remco lost 36 seconds to DSM, dramatically more than to ROGLIC/VINGEGAARD, exposing Soudal's wet TTT weakness relative to GC rivals.
Multiple teams crashed in final two corners before finish, indicating recce quality mattered more than raw power in 17-minute effort.

rider_signals.MILESI Lorenzo: positive: took red jersey after DSM's dominant TTT performance, gaining time on all GC contenders in treacherous conditions
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: visibly furious at finish, pointing at darkness, suggesting psychological fragility when conditions deviate from controlled preparation
rider_signals.MOSS Enrique: positive: sprinted first across line for MovieStar after Tour de France stage 1 crash recovery, showing strong form return
rider_signals.BARTA Roman: neutral: DSM leadership presence noted but no individual tactical signal in TTT format
